title: prune() defined but never called — rate limiter leaks memory
description:
ratelimit.rs defines prune() to evict stale per-IP buckets, but it is never called from anywhere in the codebase. Each unique source IP that hits /inbox grows the HashMap permanently. Under any sustained volume of federation traffic (botnets, crawlers, rotating IPs) this is an unbounded memory leak.

Fix: call prune() periodically from the delivery worker loop or spawn a dedicated cleanup task in server::serve. The prune threshold of 1 hour is already defined correctly.
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
